accept: Either a brew tap or a curl|sh installer exists and works on macOS arm64 + linux amd64 against an existing published release.
Install path verifies checksums against the release artifact.
Documented in README and in act help, and the CC Web bootstrap snippet (see other distribution issue) uses this path when available.
payload.description:
Per the brief: 'brew install act via homebrew tap; curl -fsSL ... | sh install script for everywhere else'. Currently the only install path is 'go build' or downloading a binary from the (still-draft) GitHub Release. Alpha-trial usage would be smoother with one of:

- A homebrew tap at github.com/aac/homebrew-act (or under a personal tap) — cleanest for macOS users.
- A curl|sh install script hosted at e.g. github.com/aac/act/releases/latest/download/install.sh that downloads the right platform binary, verifies the checksum, and drops it on PATH.

Both depend on the GitHub Release being published (not draft). That's gated on the project being meaningfully ready for outside use — see act-evaluation for the readiness read.
payload.title: publish a brew tap or curl-installer for act
